title: Open hole positioning editor directly on the OcarinaVisual model

requirements.summary: Add a 'Position Holes' button near OcarinaVisual in the main App layout for direct access to hole positioning without opening Settings modal
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Position Holes' button is visible in the main interface near the OcarinaVisual component.
The button is accessible without opening the Settings modal.
Clicking the button opens the HolePositioningEditor component.

file_operations.description: Add state to control HolePositioningEditor modal visibility. Add a 'Position Holes' button immediately below the OcarinaVisual component in the main layout. Wire the button's onClick handler to open the HolePositioningEditor as a modal or overlay. Ensure the button is styled consistently with the existing UI using shadcn/ui Button component.

requirements.summary: Open HolePositioningEditor as a modal/overlay on OcarinaVisual when 'Position Holes' is clicked, allowing drag-and-drop repositioning of all 4 holes with Save and Cancel actions
acceptanceCriteria: The HolePositioningEditor opens overlaying or directly adjacent to the OcarinaVisual component.
The editor shows the same background image currently used by OcarinaVisual (stock image or user-uploaded photo).
All 4 holes are draggable (mouse and touch) within the editor.
A Save/Set button confirms and persists the new hole positions via the useCustomHolePositions hook.
A Cancel or Close button discards changes and closes the editor.
After saving, the OcarinaVisual component immediately reflects the updated hole positions.

file_operations.description: Conditionally render the HolePositioningEditor component when the modal state is open. Pass the current ocarina photo (from useOcarinaPhoto hook), body shape, and hole positions (from useCustomHolePositions hook) as props to HolePositioningEditor. Wire the onSave callback to persist positions via useCustomHolePositions.setHolePosition and close the modal. Wire the onCancel/onClose callback to close the modal without saving. Ensure the modal overlays the main content using a Dialog or Sheet component from shadcn/ui.

Ensure the component accepts photo, bodyShape, and current hole positions as props. Update the background rendering logic to use the passed photo prop (user-uploaded or stock image frontend/public/assets/generated/ocarina-stock-grey.dim_400x400.png). Verify that the existing drag-and-drop logic (mouse and touch support) remains intact. Ensure the Save button calls the parent-provided onSave callback with the updated hole positions. Ensure the Cancel/Close button calls the parent-provided onCancel callback. Do not modify the core drag-and-drop or Save/Reset functionality.

requirements.summary: Ensure HolePositioningEditor uses the stock grey ocarina image as default background when no user photo is uploaded, matching OcarinaVisual's current display
acceptanceCriteria: When no user photo is uploaded, the HolePositioningEditor shows the stock ocarina image as background.
When a user photo is uploaded, the HolePositioningEditor shows the user photo as background.
The hole overlay markers are positioned using the current values from useCustomHolePositions.

file_operations.description: When rendering HolePositioningEditor, pass the photo prop from useOcarinaPhoto.photo. If photo is null or undefined, pass the workspace path frontend/public/assets/generated/ocarina-stock-grey.dim_400x400.png as a fallback. Ensure the same logic is used by OcarinaVisual for consistency.

Update the background image rendering to accept the photo prop and display it as the background. If the photo prop is the stock image path, render it as a static asset. If the photo prop is a user-uploaded data URL, render it directly. Ensure the hole markers overlay the background correctly using current hole positions from props. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
